<<Citation Intent Analysis>>

The citation sentence indicates that the Q-SEA instrument has been previously published. This suggests that the paper is referencing prior work. The mention of a preliminary version implies that the current paper is building upon existing research.

* * *

The use of the word "previously" points to a prior publication. The citation is not about methodology or findings but about the existence of a prior work. This context helps determine the intent behind the citation.

* * *

The citation serves to acknowledge prior work. The focus is on the publication status of the Q-SEA instrument. The intent is to provide background by referencing an earlier publication.
